mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market slack suppresses consumer spending growth and corporate earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 6.0, or signal loses predictive power with t-stat below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and stable consumer prices indicates robust demand without inflationary pressure, which expands profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in multivariate regression, or positive return frequency drops below 50 percent when both conditions align.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity buying pressure and positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during late-cycle regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with industrial production changes over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict equity direction with a Rank IC above 0.03 over rolling 24-month windows, or positive return frequency falls below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 220.0, accelerating inflation forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets continue to rally with positive 60-day returns in greater than 65 percent of threshold-crossing events, or inflation fails to predict returns after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with falling unemployment indicates a synchronized recovery in household balance sheets and labor markets, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns through improved consumer confidence.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 55 percent of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 1.5 percent annualized, corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded and risk premia rise, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Decelerating GDP growth coincides with positive equity returns in greater than 60 percent of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy with Rank IC below 0.02 during recessionary periods.
